editionData.title: July 16, 2026: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Trump threatens wider attack; Police round up Hong Kong booksellers as China widens crackdown
editionData.description: Global headlines: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Trump threatens wider attack (The latest round of attacks comes amid continued threats by Trump to “knock out” Iran’s power plants and bridges unless Tehran returns to the negotiating table); Police round up Hong Kong booksellers as China widens crackdown (After pro-democracy protests roiled the city in 2019, Beijing cracked down on freedom of speech, and restrictions have continued to tighten); and Trump and Iran signed a truce but still can’t agree on what its terms mean (Ambiguous language on who controls the Strait of Hormuz has pushed the United States and Iran back toward war). Markets leaned higher, with 5 of 7 tracked indexes up; Nikkei 225 down 2.94% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: Guerrilla London Bus Ads Mock Kylie Jenner's Meta Glasses Campaign (“Billionaires could fund cures for cancer — so why are they funding glasses for perverts instead?" asked the protest group Everyone Hates...); If you want to create a button from scratch, you must first create the universe (madcampos.dev: If you want to create a button from scratch, you must first create the universe); and Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model (Our first open-weights model: multimodal, Mixture-of-Experts, with controllable reasoning effort. Available to fine-tune on Tinker). Product Hunt features Jam-Pod (A player for people who keep their own music collection), QuickQuill (Private, on-device meeting notes for Mac), and Clerk | AI Assistant for Cap Tables (Issue grants, model funding rounds, ask it anything equity).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Trump threatens wider attack
headlines.summary: The latest round of attacks comes amid continued threats by Trump to “knock out” Iran’s power plants and bridges unless Tehran returns to the negotiating table.

headlines.title: Police round up Hong Kong booksellers as China widens crackdown
headlines.summary: After pro-democracy protests roiled the city in 2019, Beijing cracked down on freedom of speech, and restrictions have continued to tighten.

headlines.title: Trump and Iran signed a truce but still can’t agree on what its terms mean
headlines.summary: Ambiguous language on who controls the Strait of Hormuz has pushed the United States and Iran back toward war.

headlines.title: To quell graft allegations, Nigel Farage runs against a man wearing a trash can
headlines.summary: The British ally of President Donald Trump wanted a vote of confidence. Instead, other parties dumped their campaigns, and a comedian stepped in.

headlines.title: Trump retreats from shipping toll in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: As European markets fell and oil prices hit a one-month high, President Donald Trump declared on Truth Social that the strait “is open to ALL Ship traffic except for Iran.”

headlines.title: Senior Chinese delegation visits North Korea for talks
headlines.summary: China's fourth-highest-ranked official, Wang Huning, held talks with a top official of North Korea's governing party.

headlines.title: Toronto engulfed by wildfire smoke as US cities threatened
headlines.summary: Monitor ranks Toronto as having the worst air quality on earth, surpassing Kinshasa, DR Congo, and New Delhi, India.

headlines.title: Two killed in Russian missile fire on Kyiv after Ukraine-EU drone deal
headlines.summary: Mayor Vitali Klitschko says emergency services are battling blazes across the Ukrainian capital.

headlines.title: France’s Morocco reset: What French PM Lecornu’s visit to Rabat signals
headlines.summary: The trip highlights how Paris and Rabat are turning a diplomatic rapprochement into a broader strategic partnership.

headlines.title: US bombs Iran, disables oil tanker; Tehran claims attacks on Kuwait, Jordan
headlines.summary: Iranian media report explosions on Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas and Chabahar, as Bahrain and Kuwait sound air raid sirens.

headlines.title: Fear and hope in Utah in the shadow of America's largest wildfire
headlines.summary: People in Monticello, Utah have been tense and preparing to evacuate since shortly after the Babylon fire started June 26th

headlines.title: Argentina is back in the World Cup final after a thrilling semifinal win over England
headlines.summary: Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.

headlines.title: Takeaways from Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing for attorney general
headlines.summary: Todd Blanche remained composed throughout the hours-long hearing, but faced several testy moments during questioning as he seeks to win the support of all Republican senators on the committee.

headlines.title: China and Xi favored over U.S. and Trump in many nations: Survey
headlines.summary: The change is driven by improved perceptions of China and declining views of the United States.

headlines.title: Trump relished in being compared to dictators like Hitler and Stalin, journalist says
headlines.summary: The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.

headlines.title: Tensions spur ultra-rich to diversify across Asia
headlines.summary: Investor jitters over Gulf tensions are prompting wealthy families and founders to consider shifting capital to rival financial hubs, say bankers and advisers.

headlines.title: Power back in Cuba after latest blackout amid oil blockade
headlines.summary: HAVANA — Cuba has restored its power grid, authorities said on Wednesday, after the third total blackout in less than 10 days as the island faces an energy crisis amid a US oil blockade.

headlines.title: EU Council agrees position on 2027 draft budget
headlines.summary: BRUSSELS -- The Council of the European Union (EU) on Wednesday agreed its position on the EU's draft annual budget for 2027, the seventh and final annual budget under the bloc's 2021-2027 multiannual financial framework.

headlines.title: European companies unveil missile shield plan
headlines.summary: Five European defense companies have agreed to jointly develop a sovereign exo‑atmospheric interceptor – a system they say is designed to destroy medium‑range and intermediate‑range ballistic missiles while they are still in space.

headlines.title: Beijing's peace efforts draw praise
headlines.summary: China has emerged as one of the leading international actors advocating political solutions, fostering mutual understanding, and promoting dialogue in the Middle East amid escalating crises and the rapid transformations reshaping the international order, former Egyptian foreign minister Mohammed al-Orabi said.

headlines.title: Nagpur Cyber Police register FIR against content creators over alleged defamatory campaign targeting Nitin Gadkari
headlines.summary: Nagpur Cyber Police file FIR against content creators for allegedly defaming Nitin Gadkari over controversial E20 fuel policy posts.

headlines.title: In a first, marshy Sundarbans to get fenced soon; BSF to expedite work with other govt depts.
headlines.summary: The BSF plans to fence 90 km of the Sundarbans border for national security, addressing challenges in protected areas.

headlines.title: ED files PMLA complaint in alleged visa fraud racket
headlines.summary: The ED’s probe is based on multiple FIRs alleging conspiracy in the preparation of forged educational certificates, fabricated experience certificates, fake financial statements, and fraudulent proof of funds for securing U.S. student and visitor visas

headlines.title: Supreme Court rejects plea to curb reels, podcasts; warns against politicising Article 32
headlines.summary: Supreme Court denies petition to restrict social media content, emphasizing the need to avoid politicising Article 32 rights.

headlines.title: Latur coaching owner paid ₹5 lakh to procure NEET questions from NTA paper-setter, says CBI
headlines.summary: CBI arrests Latur coaching owner for allegedly paying ₹5 lakh to obtain NEET questions, linking him to a major paper leak.

headlines.title: TSMC's second-quarter profit seen hitting record on AI boom
headlines.summary: Reuters: TSMC's second-quarter profit seen hitting record on AI boom.

headlines.title: BOK hikes rates for first time in 3-1/2 years, signals more
headlines.summary: Reuters: BOK hikes rates for first time in 3-1/2 years, signals more.

headlines.title: Fewer vessels travel through Hormuz as US, Iran continue strikes
headlines.summary: Reuters: Fewer vessels travel through Hormuz as US, Iran continue strikes.

headlines.title: EXCLUSIVE: US strikes on Iran strengthen Trump's options for new escalation, officials say
headlines.summary: Reuters: EXCLUSIVE: US strikes on Iran strengthen Trump's options for new escalation, officials say.

headlines.title: Senior Chinese and North Korean officials hold talks in Pyongyang, KCNA reports
headlines.summary: Reuters: Senior Chinese and North Korean officials hold talks in Pyongyang, KCNA reports.

hackerNews.title: Grok Build is open source
hackerNews.summary: SpaceXAI's coding agent harness and TUI. Fullscreen, mouse interactive, extensible. - xai-org/grok-build

hackerNews.title: Bluesky Trademarks ATProto
hackerNews.summary: Bluesky now owns the AT Protocol trademark to help make sure it can be used as widely and responsibly as possible.

hackerNews.title: My Throw Decides My Aim
hackerNews.summary: A D-A-D song, heard as a blues sung by a large language model. On generating your intention after the fact, the voice with no throat behind it, and what interpretability keeps finding.
